## Easykube

Type-hintable wrapper for the exposed Easykube Go functions

### EASYKUBE\_BINARY\_COMPAT

Defines semver constraint for the easykube go-binary, patch versions are free to roll.
The go binary will extract this, and compare it to its own version, if there is a version
mismatch, the status command will complain.

### kustomize

Runs the kustomize tool, saves the rendered manifests into a .out file, and applies it with kubectl

Returns **[Easykube][1]**&#x20;

### helmTemplate

Renders a Helm chart to .out, remember to apply the result

#### Parameters

*   `chart` **[string][35]** relative path to the chart directory
*   `values` **[string][35]** the values file to use
*   `destination` **[string][35]** output file for rendered template
*   `namespace` **[string][35]** sets namespace in templated output (optional, default `'default'`)
*   `nametemplate` **[string][35]** sets release name in templated output (optional, default `''`)

### waitForDeployment

Waits until the specified deployment reaches ready state in the cluster.

#### Parameters

*   `deployment` **[string][35]** Deployment to wait for
*   `namespace` **[string][35]** Where the deployment lives

Returns **[Easykube][1]**&#x20;

### thenApply

Applies a single yaml resource with kubectl

#### Parameters

*   `manifest` **[string][35]** The yaml file to apply the path must be relative to the addon script

Returns **[Easykube][1]**&#x20;

### preload

Preloads images by pulling them retagging and pushing them to your local registry,
this saves bandwidth, and will make subsequent installations faster

#### Parameters

*   `images` **[Map][36]<[string][35], [string][35]>**&#x20;

Returns **[Easykube][1]**&#x20;

### runCommand

Runs a command in a container

#### Parameters

*   `deployment` **[string][35]** Name of the deployment (If A deployment has more than one container, the first discovered becomes the target)
*   `namespace` **[string][35]** , args) {
*   `command` **[string][35]** The command to run, example "ls" or "/usr/local/bin/whatever"
*   `args` **[Array][37]<[string][35]>** Arguments to the command, example \["-la","-v"]

Returns **[string][35]**&#x20;

### waitForCustomResourceDefinition

Waits until a custom resource definition appears (Operators exemplifies this issue, it can
take some time for an operator to apply its own resources)

#### Parameters

*   `group` **[string][35]** group name of crd 'acme.io'
*   `version` **[string][35]** V1
*   `kind` **[string][35]** the kind of resource, 'Widget'
*   `timeout` **[number][38]** duration in seconds to wait before giving up

Returns **[Easykube][1]**&#x20;

### copyFile

Copies a local file to a container in a pod. This is done by best effort. A pod can have many containers. If
two (or more) containers exist which matches the partial container name, the first becomes the target.

Limitation; The destination MUST be aimed at a deployment, you cannot target a bare container. This is
why you must specify a deployment name

#### Parameters

*   `deployment` **[string][35]** Name of deployment
*   `namespace` **[string][35]** Where the target deployment lives
*   `containerLike` **[string][35]** The partial name of the target container
*   `localPath` **[string][35]** Source file, relative to the \*.ek.js module definition
*   `remotePath` **[string][35]** Destination file in container, destination must be writeable

Returns **[Easykube][1]**&#x20;

### createSecret

Creates a secret in a given namespace

#### Parameters

*   `namespace` **[string][35]** create the secret in this namespace
*   `name` **[string][35]** what to call the secret
*   `data` &#x20;

### getSecret

#### Parameters

*   `namespace` &#x20;
*   `name` &#x20;

### processExternalSecrets

Preprocesses an addon's yaml output and the resources related to the ExternalSecretOperator.
Secrets will be created without ESO installed, but ESO's custom resource definitions needs to be
available.

#### Parameters

*   `secretMap` **[Map][36]<[string][35], [Map][36]<[string][35], any>>** An arbitrary map which provides the actual keys for the secret
*   `namespace` **[string][35]** where to apply the secret
*   `manifest` **[string][35]** which output to process, by default, it's the output from kustomize (.out.yaml) (optional, default `'.out.yaml'`)

### kv

Gets a value from the passed kv easykube commandline argument (easykube add fooaddon --kv foo=bar, bing=baz)

#### Parameters

*   `key` **[string][35]** in the map

Returns **[string][35]** value or undefined if key does not exist

### env

Fetch the value of an environment variable

#### Parameters

*   `key` **[string][35]**&#x20;

Returns **[string][35]** value of env-var, undefined if non-existent

### http

Performs http requests using a very basic client

#### Parameters

*   `url` **[string][35]** endpoint for request
*   `method` **[string][35]** the method, POST, GET, etc.
*   `header` **[string][35]** headers for the request
*   `body` **[Object][39]** arbitrary json structure or text

Returns **HttpResponse**&#x20;

### exec

Executes an arbitrary OS command, this calls in to go's os.Exec

#### Parameters

*   `command` **[string][35]** the command
*   `args` **[Array][37]<[string][35]>** arguments for the command

Returns **ExecResult** the result

### addonDir

Gets the directory of the current addon being evaluated

Returns **[string][35]** absolute path to the addon directory

### addonDirForName

Gets the directory of the current addon

#### Parameters

*   `name` **[string][35]** optional addon name, if specificed will return the path to another addon in the respository

Returns **[string][35]** absolute path to the addon directory
